Name: add
Description: The sum ({rA}) + ({rB}) is placed into {rD}

Name: addc
Description: The sum ({rA}) + ({rB}) is placed into {rD}

Name: adde
Description: The sum ({rA}) + ({rB}) + XER[CA] is placed into {rD}

Name: addi
Description: The sum ({rA}) + sign extended {simm} is placed into {rD}

Name: addic
Description: The sum ({rA}) + sign extended {simm} is placed into {rD}

Name: addic.
Description: The sum ({rA}) + sign extended {simm} is placed into {rD}

Name: addis
Description: The sum ({rA}) + ({uimm} << 16) is placed into {rD}

Name: addme
Description: The sum ({rA}) + {XER[CA]} + 0xFFFF_FFFF is placed into {rD}

Name: addze
Description: The sum ({rA}) + {XER[CA]} is placed into {rD}

Name: and
Description: The contents of {rS} are Bitwise ANDed with the contents of {rB} and the result is placed into {rA}

Name: andc
Description: The contents of {rS} are Bitwise ANDed with the one's complement of the contents of {rB} and the result is placed into {rA}

Name: andi.
Description: The contents of {rS} are ANDed with ({uimm} & 0xFFFF) and the result is placed into {rA}

Name: andis.
Description: The contents of {rS} are ANDed with ({uimm} << 16) and the result is placed into {rA}

Name: b
Description: {LI} specifies the branch target address.

Name: bc
Description: {BD} specifies the branch target address. {BO} and {BI} are bits specifying conditions.

Name: bcctr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Count Register if Condition
Description: {CTR} specifies the branch target address. {BO}, {BI}, and {BH} specify the bits in the condition register to be used as the condition of the branch.

Name: bclr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Link Register if Condition
Description: {LR} specifies the target address. {BO}, {BI}, and {BH} specify the bits in the condition register to be used as the condition of the branch.

Name: bctr
Description: Branch to the value in the {CTR}.

Name: bdnz
Description: Decrements the value in the {CTR}. {BD} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if {CTR} is not zero.

Name: bdnzf
DescriptiveName: Branch if Decrement Not Zero and Condition False
Description: Decrements the value in the {CTR}. {BD} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if {CTR} is not zero and Condition {BI} is false.

Name: bdnzflr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Link Register if Decrement Not Zero and Condition False
Description: Decrements the value in the {CTR}. {LR} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if {CTR} is not zero and Condition {BI} is false.

Name: bdnzlr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Link Register if Decrement Not Zero
Description: Decrements the value in the {CTR}. {LR} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if {CTR} is not zero.

Name: bdnzt
DescriptiveName: Branch if Decrement Not Zero and Condition True
Description: Decrements the value in the {CTR}. {BD} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if {CTR} is not zero and Condition {BI} is true.

Name: bdnztlr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Link Register if Decrement Not Zero and Condition True
Description: Decrements the value in the {CTR}. {LR} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if {CTR} is not zero and Condition {BI} is true.

Name: bdz
Description: Decrements the value in the {CTR}. {BD} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if the {CTR} is zero.

Name: bdzf
DescriptiveName: Branch if Decrement Zero and Condition False
Description: Decrements the value in the {CTR}. {BD} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if {CTR} is zero and Condition {BI} is false.

Name: bdzflr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Link Register if Decrement Not Zero and Condition False
Description: Decrements the value in the {CTR}. {LR} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if {CTR} is zero and Condition {BI} is false.

Name: bdzlr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Link Register if Decrement Zero
Description: Decrements the value in the {CTR}. {LR} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if the {CTR} is zero.

Name: bdzt
DescriptiveName: Branch if Decrement Zero and Condition True
Description: Decrements the value in the {CTR}. {BD} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if {CTR} is zero and Condition {BI} is true.

Name: bdztlr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Link Register if Decrement Zero and Condition True
Description: Decrements the value in the {CTR}. {LR} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if {CTR} is zero and Condition {BI} is true.

Name: beq
Description: {BD} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[EQ]} flag is set.

Name: beqctr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Count Register if Equal
Description: Branch to the value in the {CTR} if the {crfS[EQ]} flag is set.

Name: beqlr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Link Register if Equal
Description: {LR} specifies the target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[EQ]} flag is set.

Name: bge
DescriptiveName: Branch if Greater Than or Equal
Description: {BD} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[GT]} flag is set, or the {crfS[EQ]} flag is set.

Name: bgectr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Count Register if Greater Than or Equal
Description: Branch to the value in the {CTR} if the {crfS[GT]} or {crfS[EQ]} flag is set.

Name: bgelr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Link Register if Greater Than or Equal
Description: The {LR} specifies the target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[GT]} flag is set, or the {crfS[EQ]} flag is set.

Name: bgt
Description: {BD} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[GT]} flag is set.

Name: bgtctr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Count Register if Greater Than
Description: Branch to the value in the {CTR} if the {crfS[GT]} flag is set.

Name: bgtlr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Link Register if Greater Than
Description: The {LR} specifies the target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[GT]} flag is set.

Name: ble
DescriptiveName: Branch if Less Than or Equal
Description: {BD} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[LT]} flag is set, or the {crfS[EQ]} flag is set.

Name: blectr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Count Register if Less Than or Equal
Description: Branch to the value in the {CTR} if the {crfS[LT]} or {crfS[EQ]} flag is set.

Name: blelr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Link Register if Less Than or Equal
Description: The {LR} specifies the target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[LT]} flag is set, or the {crfS[EQ]} flag is set.

Name: blr
Description: The {LR} specifies the branch target address.

Name: blt
Description: {BD} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[LT]} flag is set.

Name: bltctr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Count Register if Less Than
Description: Branch to the value in the {CTR} if the {crfS[LT]} flag is set.

Name: bltlr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Link Register if Less Than
Description: The {LR} specifies the target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[LT]} flag is set.

Name: bne
Description: {BD} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[EQ]} flag is not set.

Name: bnectr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Count Register if Not Equal
Description: Branch to the value in the {CTR} if the {crfS[EQ]} flag is not set.

Name: bnelr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Link Register if Not Equal
Description: The {LR} specifies the target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[EQ]} flag is not set.

Name: bns
Description: {BD} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[SO]} flag is not set.

Name: bnsctr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Count Register if Not Summary Overflow
Description: Branch to the value in the {CTR} if the {crfS[SO]} flag is not set.

Name: bnslr
DescriptiveName: Branch To Link Register if Not Summary Overflow
Description: The {LR} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[SO]} flag is not set.

Name: bso
Description: {BD} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[SO]} flag is set.

Name: bsoctr
DescriptiveName: Branch to Count Register if Summary Overflow
Description: Branch to the value in the {CTR} if the {crfS[SO]} flag is set.

Name: bsolr
DescriptiveName: Branch To Link Register if Summary Overflow
Description: The {LR} specifies the branch target address, which will only occur if the {crfS[SO]} flag is set.

Name: clrlslwi
DescriptiveName: Clear Left Shift Left Word Immediate
Description: equivalent to rlwinm {rA}, {rS}, {SH}, {MB}-{SH}, 31-{SH}

Name: clrlwi
Description: equivalent to rlwinm {rA}, {rS}, 0, {MB}, 31

Name: clrrwi
Description: equivalent to rlwinm {rA}, {rS}, 0, 0, 31-{ME}

Name: cmp
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared with the contents of {rB}, treating the operands as signed integers. The result of the comparison is placed into CR field {crfD}. {L} is used to signify the type of comparison.

Name: cmpd
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared with the contents of {rB}, treating the operands as douhble word integers. The result of the comparison is placed into CR field {crfD}

Name: cmpdi
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared with the sign extended {simm}, treating the operands as douhble word integers. The result of the comparison is placed into CR field {crfD}

Name: cmpi
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared with the sign-extended value of {simm}, treating the operands as signed integers. The result of the comparison is placed into CR field {crfD}. {L} is used to signify the type of comparison.

Name: cmpl
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared with the contents of {rB}, treating the operands as unsigned integers. The result of the comparison is placed into CR field {crfD}. {L} is used to signify the type of comparison.

Name: cmpld
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared with the contents of {rB}, treating the operands as unsigned double word integers. The result of the comparison is placed into CR field {crfD}

Name: cmpldi
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared with ({uimm} & 0xFFFF), treating the operands as unsigned double word integers. The result of the comparison is placed into CR field {crfD}

Name: cmpli
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared with ({uimm} & 0xFFFF), treating the operands as unsigned integers. The result of the comparison is placed into CR field {crfD}. {L} is used to signify the type of comparison.

Name: cmplw
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared with the contents of {rB}, treating the operands as unsigned integers. The result of the comparison is placed into CR field {crfD}

Name: cmplwi
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared with the value of ({uimm} & 0xFFFF), treating the operands as unsigned integers. The result of the comparison is placed into CR field {crfD}

Name: cmpw
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared with the contents of {rB}, treating the operands as signed integers. The result of the comparison is placed into CR field {crfD}

Name: cmpwi
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared with the sign-extended value of {simm}, treating the operands as signed integers. The result of the comparison is placed into CR field {crfD}

Name: cntlzw
Description: A count of the number of consecutive zero bits starting at bit 0 of {rS} is placed into {rA}. This number ranges from 0 to 32, inclusive.

Name: crand
Description: The bit in the condition register {crbA} is ANDed with the bit in the condition register {crbB}. The result is placed into the condition register {crbD}

Name: crandc
Description: The bit in the condition register {crbA} is ANDed with the complement of the bit in the condition register {crbB} and the result is placed into the condition register bit {crbD}

Name: crclr
Description: Clears all flags in the condition register {crbD}

Name: creqv
Description: The bit in the condition register {crbA} is XORed with the bit in the condition register {crbB} and the complemented result is placed into the condition register bit {crbD}

Name: crmove
Description: Moves the contents of the Condition Register {crbA} into Condition Register {crbD}.

Name: crnand
Description: The bit in the condition register {crbA} is ANDed with the bit in the condition register {crbB} and the complemented result is placed into the condition register bit {crbD}

Name: crnor
Description: The bit in the condition register {crbA} is ORed with the bit in the condition register {crbB} and the complemented result is placed into the condition register bit {crbD}

Name: crnot
Description: The bit in the condition register {crbA} is inverted and placed into the condition register bit {crbD}

Name: cror
Description: The bit in the condition register {crbA} is ORed with the bit in the condition register {crbB} and the complemented result is placed into the condition register bit {crbD}

Name: crorc
Description: The bit in the condition register {crbA} is ORed with the complement of the condition register bit {crbB} and the result is placed into the condition register bit {crbD}

Name: crset
Description: Sets the bit in the Condition Register {crbD}

Name: crxor
Description: The bit in the condition register {crbA} is XORed with the bit in the condition register {crbB} and the result is placed into the condition register {crbD}

Name: dcbf
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The dcbf instruction invalidates the block in the data cache addressed by EA, copying the block to memory first, if there is any dirty data in it. Unmodified block - Invalidates the block in the processor's data cache

Name: dcbi
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The action taken is dependent on the memory mode associated with the block containing the byte addressed by EA and on the state of that block. The list below describes the action taken if the block containing the byte addressed by EA is or is not in the cache. Unmodified block -> Invalidates the block in the processor's data cache. Modified block -> Invalidates the block in the processor's data cache. (Discards the modified contents.) Absent block (target block not in cache) -> No action is taken

Name: dcbst
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). If the block containing the byte addressed by EA is in coherency-not-required mode, and a block containing the byte addressed by EA is in the data cache of this processor and has been modified, the writing of it to main memory is initiated

Name: dcbt
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). This instruction is a hint that performance will possibly be improved if the block containing the byte addressed by EA is fetched into the data cache, because the program will probably soon load from the addressed byte

Name: dcbtst
DescriptiveName: Data Cache Block Touch for Store
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). This instruction is a hint that performance will possibly be improved if the block containing the byte addressed by EA is fetched into the data cache, because the program will probably soon store from the addressed byte

Name: dcbz
DescriptiveName: Data Cache Block Clear to Zero
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). This instruction is treated as a store to the addressed byte with respect to address translation, memory protection, referenced and changed recording. It is also treated as a store with respect to the ordering enforced by {eieio} and the ordering enforced by the combination of caching-inhibited and guarded attributes for a page (or block)

Name: dcbz_l
DescriptiveName: Data Cache Block Set to Zero Locked
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). If the cache block containing the byte addressed by EA is neither in the "locked" nor in the "normal" data cache, the block is allocated in the "locked" data cache without fetching the block from main memory. All bytes are cleared and the block is marked as M (modified). Cache block allocation is done using the psudo-LRU used rule among the four ways in the locked cache

Name: divw
Description: The dividend is the contents of {rA}. The divisor is the contents of {rB}. The remainder is not supplied as a result. Both the operands and the quotient are interpreted as signed integers. The quotient is the unique signed integer that satisfies the equation -> dividend = (quotient x divisor) + r where 0 <= r < |divisor| (if the dividend is non-negative), and -|divisor| < r <= 0 (if the dividend is negative). The result of the division is stored in {rD}.

Name: divwu
Description: The dividend is the contents of {rA}. The divisor is the contents of {rB}. The remainder is not supplied as a result. Both operands and the quotient are interpreted as unsigned integers, except that if {Rc} = 1 the first three bits of CR0 field are set by signed comparison of the result to zero. The quotient is the unique unsigned integer that satisfies the equation-dividend = (quotient * divisor) + r (where 0 <= r < divisor). The result of the division is stored in {rD}.

Name: eciwx
Description: The eciwx instruction and the {EAR} register can be very efficient when mapping special devices such as graphics devices that use addresses as pointers. EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). A load word request for the physical address (referred to as real address in the architecture specification) corresponding to EA is sent to the device identified by {EAR[RID]}, bypassing the cache. The word returned by the device is placed in {rD}

Name: ecowx
Description: The ecowx instruction and the EAR register can be very efficient when mapping special devices such as graphics devices that use addresses as pointers. EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). A store word request for the physical address corresponding to EA and the contents of {rS} are sent to the device identified by {EAR[RID]}, bypassing the cache.

Name: eieio
Description: The eieio instruction provides an ordering function for the effects of load and store instructions executed by a processor. These loads and stores are divided into two sets, which are ordered separately. The memory accesses caused by a {dcbz} or a {dcba} instruction are ordered like a store.

Name: eqv
Description: The contents of {rS} are XORed with the contents of {rB} and the complemented result is placed into {rA}

Name: extlwi
Description: equivalent to rlwinm {rA}, {rS}, {SH}, 0, {ME}-1

Name: extrwi
Description: equivalent to rlwinm {rA}, {rS}, {SH} + {MB}, 32-{MB}, 31

Name: extsb
Description: The contents of the low-order eight bits of {rS} are placed into the low-order eight bits of {rA}. Bit 24 of {rS} is placed into the remaining bits of {rA}

Name: extsh
Description: The contents of the low-order 16 bits of {rS} are placed into the low-order 16 bits of {rA}[16-31]. Bit 16 of {rS} is placed into the remaining bits of {rA}

Name: fabs
Description: The contents of {frB} with bit 0 cleared are placed into {frD}

Name: fadd
Description: The floating-point operand in {frA} is added to the floating-point operand in {frB}. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to double-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}

Name: fadds
Description: The floating-point operand in {frA} is added to the floating-point operand in {frB}. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to the single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}

Name: fcmpo
Description: The floating-point operand in {frA} is compared to the floating-point operand in {frB}. The result of the compare is placed into CR field {crfD} and the {FPCC}

Name: fcmpu
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA} is compared to the floating-point operand in register {frB}. The result of the compare is placed into CR field {crfD} and the {FPCC}

Name: fctiw
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frB} is converted to a 32-bit signed integer, using the rounding mode specified by {FPSCR[RN]}, and placed in bits 32-63 of {frD}. Bits 0-31 of {frD} are undefined. If the operand in {frB} are greater than 2^31 - 1, bits 32-63 of {frD} are set to 0x7FFF_FFFF. If the operand in {frB} are less than -2^31 , bits 32-63 of {frD} are set to 0x8000_0000.

Name: fctiwz
DescriptiveName: Floating Convert to Integer Word with Round toward Zero
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frB} is converted to a 32-bit signed integer, using the rounding mode round toward zero, and placed in bits 32-63 of {frD}. Bits 0-31 of {frD} are undefined. If the operand in {frB} are greater than 2^31 - 1, bits 32-63 of {frD} are set to 0x7FFF_FFFF. If the operand in {frB} are less than -2^31 , bits 32-63 of {frD} are set to 0x8000_0000.

Name: fdiv
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA} is divided by the floating-point operand in register {frB}. The remainder is not supplied as a result. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to double-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}.

Name: fdivs
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA} is divided by the floating-point operand in register {frB}. The remainder is not supplied as a result. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}.

Name: fmadd
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA} is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}. The floating-point operand in register {frB} is added to this intermediate result. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to double-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}.

Name: fmadds
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA} is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}. The floating-point operand in register {frB} is added to this intermediate result. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field FPSCR[RN] and placed into {frD}.

Name: fmr
Description: The content of register {frB} is placed into {frD}

Name: fmsub
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA} is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}. The floating-point operand in register {frB} is subtracted from this intermediate result. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to double-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}.

Name: fmsubs
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA} is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}. The floating-point operand in register {frB} is subtracted from this intermediate result. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}.

Name: fmul
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA} is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to double-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}.

Name: fmuls
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA} is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}.

Name: fnabs
Description: The contents of register {frB} with bit 0 set are placed into {frD}.

Name: fneg
Description: The contents of register {frB} with bit 0 inverted are placed into {frD}.

Name: fnmadd
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA} is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}. The floating-point operand in register {frB} is added to this intermediate result. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to double-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]}, then negated and placed into {frD}.

Name: fnmadds
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA} is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}. The floating-point operand in register {frB} is added to this intermediate result. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]}, then negated and placed into {frD}

Name: fnmsub
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA} is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}. The floating-point operand in register {frB} is subtracted from this intermediate result. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to double-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]}, then negated and placed into {frD}

Name: fnmsubs
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA} is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}. The floating-point operand in register {frB} is subtracted from this intermediate result. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]}, then negated and placed into {frD}

Name: fres
Description: A single-precision estimate of the reciprocal of the floating-point operand in register {frB} is placed into register {frD}.

Name: frsp
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frB} is rounded to single-precision using the rounding mode specified by {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}

Name: frsqrte
Description: A double-precision estimate of the reciprocal of the square root of the floating-point operand in register {frB} is placed into register {frD}.

Name: fsel
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA} is compared to the value zero. If the operand is greater than or equal to zero, register {frD} is set to the contents of register {frC}. If the operand is less than zero or is a NaN, register {frD} is set to the contents of register {frB}

Name: fsub
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frB} is subtracted from the floating-point operand in register {frA}. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to double-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}

Name: fsubs
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frB} is subtracted from the floating-point operand in register {frA}. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}.

Name: icbi
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). If the block containing the byte addressed by EA is in coherency-required mode, and a block containing the byte addressed by EA is in the instruction cache of any processor, the block is made invalid in all such instruction caches, so that subsequent references cause the block to be refetched

Name: isync
Description: The isync instruction provides an ordering function for the effects of all instructions executed by a processor. Executing an isync instruction ensures that all instructions preceding the isync instruction have completed before the isync instruction completes. It also ensures that no subsequent instructions are initiated by the processor until after the isync instruction completes

Name: lbz
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The byte in memory addressed by EA is loaded into the low-order eight bits of {rD}. The remaining bits in {rD} are cleared.

Name: lbzu
DescriptiveName: Load Byte and Zero with Update
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The byte in memory addressed by EA is loaded into the low-order eight bits of {rD}. The remaining bits in {rD} are cleared. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: lbzux
DescriptiveName: Load Byte and Zero with Update Indexed
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The byte in memory addressed by EA is loaded into the low-order eight bits of {rD}. The remaining bits in {rD} are cleared. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: lbzx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The byte in memory addressed by EA is loaded into the low-order eight bits of {rD}. The remaining bits in {rD} are cleared.

Name: lfd
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The double word in memory addressed by EA is placed into {frD}.

Name: lfdu
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The double word in memory addressed by EA is placed into {frD}. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: lfdux
DescriptiveName: Load Floating-Point Double with Update Indexed
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The double word in memory addressed by EA is placed into {frD}. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: lfdx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The double word in memory addressed by EA is placed into {frD}

Name: lfs
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The word in memory addressed by EA is interpreted as a single-precision floation-point and placed into {frD}

Name: lfsu
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The word in memory addressed by EA is interpreted as a single-precision floation-point and placed into {frD}. EA is placed into {rA}

Name: lfsux
DescriptiveName: Load Floating-Point Single with Update Indexed
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The word in memory addressed by EA is interpreted as a single-precision floation-point and placed into {frD}. EA is placed into {rA}

Name: lfsx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The word in memory addressed by EA is interpreted as a single-precision floation-point and placed into {frD}

Name: lha
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The half word in memory addressed by EA is loaded into the low-order 16 bits of {rD}. The remaining bits in {rD} are filled with a copy of the most-significant bit of the loaded half word.

Name: lhau
DescriptiveName: Load Half Word Algebraic with Update
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The half word in memory addressed by EA is loaded into the low-order 16 bits of {rD}. The remaining bits in {rD} are filled with a copy of the most-significant bit of the loaded half word. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: lhaux
DescriptiveName: Load Half Word Algebraic with Update Indexed
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The half word in memory addressed by EA is loaded into the low-order 16 bits of {rD}. The remaining bits in {rD} are filled with a copy of the most-significant bit of the loaded half word. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: lhax
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The half word in memory addressed by EA is loaded into the low-order 16 bits of {rD}. The remaining bits in {rD} are filled with a copy of the most-significant bit of the loaded half word.

Name: lhbrx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). Bits 0-7 of the half word in memory addressed by EA are loaded into the low-order eight bits of {rD}. Bits 8-15 of the half word in memory addressed by EA are loaded into the subsequent low-order eight bits of {rD}. The remaining bits in {rD} are cleared

Name: lhz
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The half word in memory addressed by EA is loaded into the low-order 16 bits of {rD}. The remaining bits in {rD} are cleared.

Name: lhzu
DescriptiveName: Load Half Word and Zero with Update
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The half word in memory addressed by EA is loaded into the low-order 16 bits of {rD}. The remaining bits in {rD} are cleared. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: lhzux
DescriptiveName: Load Half Word and Zero with Update Indexed
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The half word in memory addressed by EA is loaded into the low-order 16 bits of {rD}. The remaining bits in {rD} are cleared. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: lhzx
DescriptiveName: Load Half Word and Zero Indexed
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The half word in memory addressed by EA is loaded into the low-order 16 bits of {rD}. The remaining bits in {rD} are cleared. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: li
Description: The sign-extended value {simm} is placed into {rD}

Name: lis
Description: The value ({uimm} << 16) is placed into {rD}

Name: lmw
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. n = (32 - {rD}). n consecutive words starting at EA are loaded into GPRs {rD} through r31.

Name: lswi
Description: EA is ({rA}). Let n = {NB} if {NB} != 0, n = 32 if {NB} == 0; n is the number of bytes to load. Let nr = CEIL(n / 4); nr is the number of registers to be loaded with data. n consecutive bytes starting at EA are loaded into GPRs {rD} through {rD} + nr - 1.

Name: lswx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). Let n = XER[25-31]; n is the number of bytes to load. Let nr = CEIL(n / 4); nr is the number of registers to receive data. If n > 0, n consecutive bytes starting at EA are loaded into GPRs {rD} through ({rD} + nr - 1). Bytes are loaded left to right in each register. The sequence of registers wraps around through r0 if required. If the four bytes of {rD} + nr - 1 are only partially filled, the unfilled low-order byte(s) of that register are cleared. If n = 0, the contents of {rD} are undefined.

Name: lwarx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The word in memory addressed by EA is loaded into {rD}. This instruction creates a reservation for use by a store word conditional indexed ({stwcx.})instruction. The physical address computed from EA is associated with the reservation, and replaces any address previously associated with the reservation.

Name: lwbrx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {rB}. Bits 0-7 of the word in memory addressed by EA are loaded into the low-order 8 bits of {rD}. Bits 8-15 of the word in memory addressed by EA are loaded into the subsequent low-order 8 bits of {rD}. Bits 16-23 of the word in memory addressed by EA are loaded into the subsequent low-order eight bits of {rD}. Bits 24-31 of the word in memory addressed by EA are loaded into the subsequent low-order 8 bits of {rD}.

Name: lwz
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The word in memory addressed by EA is loaded into {rD}.

Name: lwzu
DescriptiveName: Load Word and Zero with Update
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The word in memory addressed by EA is loaded into {rD}. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: lwzux
DescriptiveName: Load Word and Zero with Update Indexed
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The word in memory addressed by EA is loaded into {rD}. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: lwzx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The word in memory addressed by EA is loaded into {rD}.

Name: mcrf
Description: The contents of condition register field {crfS} are copied into condition register field {crfD}. All other condition register fields remain unchanged.

Name: mcrfs
DescriptiveName: Move to Condition Register from FPSCR
Description: The contents of FPSCR field {crfS} are copied to CR field {crfD}. All exception bits copied (except {FPSCR[FEX]} and {FPSCR[VX]}) are cleared in the FPSCR.

Name: mcrxr
DescriptiveName: Move to Condition Register from XER
Description: The contents of XER[0-3] are copied into the condition register field designated by {crfD}. All other fields of the condition register remain unchanged. XER[0-3] is cleared.

Name: mfcr
Description: The contents of the condition register (CR) are placed into {rD}.

Name: mfctr
Description: The contents of the Count register are placed into {rD}.

Name: mfdar
Description: The contents of the designated special purpose register DAR are placed into {rD}.

Name: mfdbatl
Description: The contents of the DBAT{spr_BAT}L special purpose register are placed into {rD}.

Name: mfdbatu
Description: The contents of the DBAT{spr_BAT}U special purpose register are placed into {rD}.

Name: mfdec
Description: The contents of the {DEC} special purpose register are placed into {rD}.

Name: mfdsisr
Description: The contents of the {DSISR} special purpose register are placed into {rD}.

Name: mfear
Description: The contents of the EAR special purpose register are placed into {rD}.

Name: mffs
Description: The contents of the floating-point status and control register (FPSCR) are placed into the low-order bits of register {frD}. The high-order bits of register {frD} are undefined.

Name: mfibatl
Description: The contents of the IBAT{spr_BAT}L special purpose register are placed into {rD}.

Name: mfibatu
Description: The contents of the IBAT{spr_BAT}U special purpose register are placed into {rD}.

Name: mflr
Description: The contents of the {LR} are placed into {rD}.

Name: mfmsr
Description: The contents of the {MSR} are placed into {rD}.

Name: mfsdr1
Description: The contents of the {SDR1} special purpose register are placed into {rD}.

Name: mfspr
Description: The contents of the designated Special-Purpose register {spr} are placed into {rD}.

Name: mfsprg
Description: The contents of the designated special purpose register SPRG{spr_SPRG} are placed into {rD}.

Name: mfsr
Description: The contents of the Segment Register {sr} are copied into {rD}.

Name: mfsrin
Description: The contents of the segment register selected by bits 0-3 of {rB} are copied into {rD}.

Name: mfsrr0
Description: The contents of the designated special purpose register SRR0 are placed into {rD}.

Name: mfsrr1
Description: The contents of the SRR1 special purpose register are placed into {rD}.

Name: mftb
Description: The contents of TBL or TBU are copied into {rD}, as designated by the value {tbr}

Name: mfxer
Description: The contents of the Integer exception register are placed into {rD}.

Name: mr
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into {rA}.

Name: mtcrf
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the Condition Register under control of the field mask {crm}.

Name: mtctr
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the {CTR}.

Name: mtdar
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the DAR special purpose register.

Name: mtdbatl
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the DBAT{spr_BAT}L special purpose register.

Name: mtdbatu
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the DBAT{spr_BAT}U special purpose register.

Name: mtdec
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the DEC special purpose register.

Name: mtdsisr
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the DSISR special purpose register.

Name: mtear
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the EAR special purpose register.

Name: mtfsb0
Description: Bit {crbD} of the FPSCR is cleared.

Name: mtfsb1
Description: Bit {crbD} of the FPSCR is set.

Name: mtfsf
Description: The low-order 32 bits of {frB} are placed into the FPSCR under control of the field mask {mtfsf_FM}.

Name: mtfsfi
Description: The value of the {mtfsf_IMM} field is placed into FPSCR field {crfD}.

Name: mtibatl
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the IBAT{spr_BAT}L special purpose register.

Name: mtibatu
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the IBAT{spr_BAT}U special purpose register.

Name: mtlr
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the {LR}.

Name: mtmsr
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the {MSR}.

Name: mtsdr1
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the SDR1 special purpose register.

Name: mtspr
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the Special-Purpose Register {spr}.

Name: mtsprg
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the designated special purpose register SPRG{spr_SPRG}.

Name: mtsr
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into Segment Register {sr}.

Name: mtsrin
Description: The contents of {rS} are copied to the segment register selected by bits 0-3 of {rB}.

Name: mtsrr0
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the SRR0 special purpose register.

Name: mtsrr1
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the SRR1 special purpose register.

Name: mttbl
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the TBL special purpose register.

Name: mttbu
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the TBU special purpose register.

Name: mtxer
Description: The contents of {rS} are placed into the Integer exception register.

Name: mulhw
Description: The 32-bit product is formed from the contents {rA} and {rB}. The high-order 32 bits of the 64-bit product of the operands are placed into {rD}. Both the operands and the product are interpreted as signed integers.

Name: mulhwu
Description: The 32-bit operands are the contents {rA} and {rB}. The high-order 32 bits of the 64-bit product of the operands are placed into {rD}.

Name: mulli
Description: The first operand is {rA}. The second operand is {simm}. The low-order 32-bits of the 48-bit product of the operands are placed into {rD}.

Name: mullw
Description: The 32-bit operands are the contents of {rA} and {rB}. The low-order of the 64-bit product ({rA}) * ({rB}) are placed into {rD}.

Name: nand
Description: The contents of {rS} are ANDed with the contents of {rB} and the complemented result is placed into {rA}.

Name: neg
Description: The value 1 is added to the complement of the value in {rA}, and the resulting two's complement is placed into {rD}.

Name: nop
Description: Do Nothing.

Name: nor
Description: The contents of {rS} are ORed with the contents of {rB} and the complemented result is placed into {rA}.

Name: or
Description: The contents of {rS} are ORed with the contents of {rB} and the result is placed into {rA}.

Name: orc
Description: The contents of {rS} are ORed with the complement of the contents of {rB} and the result is placed into {rA}.

Name: ori
Description: The contents of {rS} are ORed with ({uimm} & 0xFFFF) and the result is placed into {rA}.

Name: oris
Description: The contents of {rS} are ORed with ({uimm} << 16) and the result is placed into {rA}.

Name: ps_abs
Description: The contents of {frB}(ps0) with bit 0 cleared are placed into {frD}(ps0). The contents of {frB}(ps1) with bit 0 cleared are placed into {frD}(ps1).

Name: ps_add
Description: The floating-point operand in {frA}(ps0) is added to the floating-point operand in {frB}(psps0). If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps0). The floating-point operand in {frA}(ps1) is added to the floating-point operand in {frB}(psps1). If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_cmpo0
Description: The floating-point operand in {frA}(ps0) is compared to the floating-point operand in {frB}(psps0). The result of the compare is placed into CR field {crfD} and the FPCC.

Name: ps_cmpo1
Description: The floating-point operand in {frA}(ps1) is compared to the floating-point operand in {frB}(psps1). The result of the compare is placed into CR field {crfD} and the FPCC.

Name: ps_cmpu0
Description: The floating-point operand in {frA}(ps0) is compared to the floating-point operand in {frB}(psps0). The result of the compare is placed into CR field {crfD} and the FPCC.

Name: ps_cmpu1
Description: The floating-point operand in {frA}(ps1) is compared to the floating-point operand in {frB}(psps1). The result of the compare is placed into CR field {crfD} and the FPCC.

Name: ps_div
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps0) is divided by the floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps0). The remainder is not supplied as a result. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps0). The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps1) is divided by the floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps1). The remainder is not supplied as a result. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_madd
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps0) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps0). The floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps0) is added to this intermediate product. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps0). The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps1) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps1). The floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps1) is added to this intermediate product. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_madds0
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps0) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps0). The floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps0) is added to this intermediate result, if the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and is placed into {frD}(psps0).

Name: ps_madds1
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps1) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps1). The floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps1) is added to this intermediate result, if the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and is placed into {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_merge00
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps0) is moved to register {frD}(psps0) and floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps0) is moved to register {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_merge01
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps0) is moved to register {frD}(psps0) and floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps1) is moved to register {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_merge10
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps1) is moved to register {frD}(psps0) and floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps0) is moved to register {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_merge11
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps1) is moved to register {frD}(psps0) and floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps1) is moved to register {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_mr
Description: The contents of register {frB}(ps0) are placed into {frD}(psps0). The contents of register {frB}(ps1) are placed into {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_msub
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps0) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps0). The floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps0) is subtracted from this intermediate product. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps0). The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps1) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps1). The floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps1) is subtracted from this intermediate product. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_mul
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps0) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps0). The floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps0) is subtracted from this intermediate product. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps0). The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps1) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps1). The floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps1) is subtracted from this intermediate product. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_muls0
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps0) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps0). If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps0). The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps1) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps0). If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_muls1
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps0) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps1). If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and are placed into {frD}(psps0). The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps1) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps1). If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and are placed into {frD}(psps1)

Name: ps_nabs
Description: The contents of register {frB}(psps0) with bit 0 set are placed into {frD}(psps0). The contents of register {frB}(psps1) with bit 0 set are placed into {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_neg
Description: The contents of register {frB}(psps0) with bit 0 inverted are placed into {frD}(psps0). The contents of register {frB}(psps1) with bit 0 inverted are placed into {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_nmadd
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps0) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps0). The floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps0) is added to this intermediate product and the result is negated. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps0). The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps1) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps1). The floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps1) is added to this intermediate product and the result is negated. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_nmsub
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps0) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps0). The floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps0) is subtracted from this intermediate product. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]}, then negated and placed into {frD}(psps0). The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps1) is multiplied by the floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps1). The floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps1) is subtracted from this intermediate product. If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]}, then negated and placed into {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_res
Description: A single-precision estimate of the reciprocal of the floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps0) is placed into register {frD}(psps0) and a single-precision estimate of the reciprocal of the floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps1) is placed into register {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_rsqrte
DescriptiveName: Paired Single Reciprocal Square Root Estimate
Description: A single-precision estimate of the reciprocal of the square root of the floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps0) is placed into register {frD}(psps0). A single-precision estimate of the reciprocal of the square root of the floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps1) is placed into register {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_sel
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps0) is compared to the value zero. If the operand is greater than or equal to zero, register {frD}(psps0) is set to the contents of register {frC}(ps0). If the operand is less than zero or is a NaN, register {frD}(psps0) is set to the contents of register {frB}(psps0). The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps1) is compared to the value zero. If the operand is greater than or equal to zero, register {frD}(psps1) is set to the contents of register {frC}(ps1). If the operand is less than zero or is a NaN, register {frD}(psps1) is set to the contents of register {frB}(psps1).

Name: ps_sub
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps0) is subtracted from the floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps0). If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps0). The floating-point operand in register {frB}(psps1) is subtracted from the floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps1). If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_sum0
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps0) is added to the floating-point operand from register {frB}(psps1). If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps0). The floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps1) is placed into {frD}(psps1).

Name: ps_sum1
Description: The floating-point operand in register {frC}(ps0) is placed into {frD}(psps0). The floating-point operand in register {frA}(ps0) is added to the floating-point operand from register {frB}(psps1). If the most-significant bit of the resultant significand is not a one, the result is normalized. The result is rounded to single-precision under control of the floating-point rounding control field {FPSCR[RN]} and placed into {frD}(psps1).

Name: psq_l
Usage: psq_l frD, ps_offset, rA, ps_W, ps_I
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {ps_offset}. PS0 and PS1 in {frD} are loaded with a pair of single precision floating point numbers from the memory addressed by EA. {ps_W} and {ps_I} are used to specify quantization information.

Name: psq_lu
DescriptiveName: Paired Single Quantized Load with Update
Usage: psq_lu frD, ps_offset, rA, ps_W, ps_I
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {ps_offset}. PS0 and PS1 in {frD} are loaded with a pair of single precision floating point numbers from the memory addressed by EA. EA is placed into {rA}. {ps_W} and {ps_I} are used to specify quantization information.

Name: psq_lux
DescriptiveName: Paired Single Quantized Load with Update Indexed
Usage: psq_lux frD, rA, rB, ps_WX, ps_IX
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). PS0 and PS1 in {frD} are loaded with a pair of single precision floating point numbers from the memory addressed by EA. EA is placed into {rA}. {ps_WX} and {ps_IX} are used to specify quantization information.

Name: psq_lx
Usage: psq_lx frD, rA, rB, ps_WX, ps_IX
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). PS0 and PS1 in {frD} are loaded with a pair of single precision floating point numbers from the memory addressed by EA. {ps_WX} and {ps_IX} are used to specify quantization information.

Name: psq_st
Usage: psq_st frS, ps_offset, rA, ps_W, ps_I
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {ps_offset}. The pair of floating point numbers from {frS}(ps0) and {frS}(ps1) are quantized and stored to memory starting at EA. {ps_W} and {ps_I} are used to specify quantization information.

Name: psq_stu
DescriptiveName: Paired Single Quantized Store with Update
Usage: psq_stu frS, ps_offset, rA, ps_W, ps_I
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {ps_offset}. The pair of floating point numbers from {frS}(ps0) and {frS}(ps1) are quantized and stored to memory starting at EA. EA is placed into {rA}. {ps_W} and {ps_I} are used to specify quantization information.

Name: psq_stux
DescriptiveName: Paired Single Quantized Store with Update Indexed
Usage: psq_stux frS, rA, rB, ps_WX, ps_IX
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The pair of floating point numbers from {frS}(ps0) and {frS}(ps1) are quantized and stored to memory starting at EA. EA is placed into {rA}. {ps_WX} and {ps_IX} are used to specify quantization information.

Name: psq_stx
Usage: psq_stx frS, rA, rB, ps_WX, ps_IX
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The pair of floating point numbers from {frS}(ps0) and {frS}(ps1) are quantized and stored to memory starting at EA. {ps_WX} and {ps_IX} are used to specify quantization information.

Name: rfi
Description: Returns from interrupt.

Name: rlwimi
DescriptiveName: Rotate Left Word Immediate then Mask Insert
Usage: rlwimi rA, rS, SH, MB, ME
Description: The contents of {rS} are rotated left the number of bits specified by operand {SH}. A mask is generated having 1 bits from bit {MB} through bit {ME} and 0 bits elsewhere. The rotated data is inserted into {rA} under control of the generated mask.

Name: rlwinm
DescriptiveName: Rotate Left Word Immediate then AND with Mask
Usage: rlwinm rA, rS, SH, MB, ME
Description: The contents of {rS} are rotated left the number of bits specified by operand {SH}. A mask is generated having 1 bits from bit {MB} through bit {ME} and 0 bits elsewhere. The rotated data is ANDed with the generated mask and the result is placed into {rA}.

Name: rlwnm
DescriptiveName: Rotate Left Word then AND with Mask
Usage: rlwnm rA, rS, rB, MB, ME
Description: The contents of {rS} are rotated left the number of bits specified by the low-order five bits of {rB}. A mask is generated having 1 bits from bit {MB} through bit {ME} and 0 bits elsewhere. The rotated data is ANDed with the generated mask and the result is placed into {rA}.

Name: rotlw
Description: equivalent to rlwnm {rA}, {rS}, {rB}, 0, 31

Name: rotlwi
Description: equivalent to rlwinm {rA}, {rS}, {SH}, 0, 31

Name: rotrwi
Description: equivalent to rlwinm {rA}, {rS}, 32-{SH}, 0, 31

Name: sc
Description: The sc instruction calls the operating system to perform a service. When control is returned to the program that executed the system call, the content of the registers depends on the register conventions used by the program providing the system service

Name: slw
Description: The contents of {rS} are shifted left the number of bits specified by the low-order five bits of {rB}. Bits shifted out of position 0 are lost. Zeros are supplied to the vacated positions on the right. The 32-bit result is placed into {rA}.

Name: slwi
Description: equivalent to rlwinm {rA}, {rS}, {SH}, 0, 31-{SH}

Name: sraw
Description: The contents of {rS} are shifted right the number of bits specified by the low-order five bits of {rB} (shift amounts between 0-31). Bits shifted out of position 31 are lost. Bit 0 of {rS} is replicated to fill the vacated positions on the left. The 32-bit result is placed into {rA}.

Name: srawi
Description: The contents of {rS} are shifted right {SH} bits. Bits shifted out of position 31 are lost. Bit 0 of {rS} is replicated to fill the vacated positions on the left. The result is placed into {rA}.

Name: srw
Description: The contents of {rS} are shifted right the number of bits specified by the low-order five bits of {rB} (shift amounts between 0-31). Bits shifted out of position 31 are lost. Zeros are supplied to the vacated positions on the left. The 32-bit result is placed into {rA}.

Name: srwi
Description: equivalent to rlwinm {rA}, {rS}, 32-{MB}, {MB}, 31

Name: stb
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The contents of the low-order eight bits of {rS} are stored into the byte in memory addressed by EA

Name: stbu
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The contents of the low-order eight bits of {rS} are stored into the byte in memory addressed by EA. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: stbux
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The contents of the low-order eight bits of {rS} are stored into the byte in memory addressed by EA. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: stbx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The contents of the low-order eight bits of {rS} are stored into the byte in memory addressed by EA.

Name: stfd
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The contents of register {frS} are stored into the double word in memory addressed by EA.

Name: stfdu
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The contents of register {frS} are stored into the double word in memory addressed by EA. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: stfdux
DescriptiveName: Store Floating-Point Double with Update Indexed
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The contents of register {frS} are stored into the double word in memory addressed by EA. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: stfdx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The contents of register {frS} are stored into the double word in memory addressed by EA.

Name: stfiwx
DescriptiveName: Store Floating-Point as Integer Word Indexed
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The contents of the low-order 32 bits of register {frS} are stored, without conversion, the word in memory addressed by EA.

Name: stfs
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The contents of register {frS} are converted to single-precision and stored into the word in memory addressed by EA.

Name: stfsu
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The contents of register {frS} are converted to single-precision and stored into the word in memory addressed by EA. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: stfsux
DescriptiveName: Store Floating-Point Single with Update Indexed
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The contents of register {frS} are converted to single-precision and stored into the word in memory addressed by EA. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: stfsx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The contents of register {frS} are converted to single-precision and stored into the word in memory addressed by EA.

Name: sth
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The contents of the low-order 16 bits of {rS} are stored into the half word in memory addressed by EA.

Name: sthbrx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The contents of the low-order eight bits (24-31) of {rS} are stored into bits 0-7 of the half word in memory addressed by EA. The contents of the subsequent low-order eight bits (16-23) of {rS} are stored into bits 8-15 of the half word in memory addressed by EA.

Name: sthu
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The contents of the low-order 16 bits of {rS} are stored into the half word in memory addressed by EA. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: sthux
DescriptiveName: Store Half Word with Update Indexed
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The contents of the low-order 16 bits of {rS} are stored into the half word in memory addressed by EA. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: sthx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The contents of the low-order 16 bits of {rS} are stored into the half word in memory addressed by EA.

Name: stmw
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. n = (32 - rS). n consecutive words starting at  EA are stored from the GPRs {rS} through r31. For example, if {rS} = 30, 2 words are stored.

Name: stswi
Description: EA is ({rA}). Let n = {NB} if {NB} != 0, n = 32 if {NB} == 0; n is the number of bytes to store. Let nr = CEIL(n / 4); nr is the number of registers to supply data. n consecutive bytes starting at EA are stored from GPRs {rS} through {rS} + nr - 1 Bytes are stored left to right from each register. The sequence of registers wraps around through r0 if required.

Name: stswx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). Let n = XER[25-31]; n is the number of bytes to store. Let nr = CEIL(n / 4); nr is the number of registers to supply data. n consecutive bytes starting at EA are stored from GPRs {rS} through {rS} + nr - 1. Bytes are stored left to right from each register. The sequence of registers wraps around through r0 if required. If n = 0, no bytes are stored.

Name: stw
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The contents of {rS} are stored into the word in memory addressed by EA.

Name: stwbrx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The contents of the low-order eight bits (24-31) of {rS} are stored into bits 0-7 of the word in memory addressed by EA. The contents of the subsequent eight low-order bits (16-23) of {rS} are stored into bits 8-15 of the word in memory addressed by EA. The contents of the subsequent eight low-order bits (8-15) of {rS} are stored into bits 16-23 of the word in memory addressed by EA. The contents of the subsequent eight low-order bits (0-7) of {rS} are stored into bits 24-31 of the word in memory addressed by EA.

Name: stwcx.
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). If the reserved bit is set, the stwcx. instruction stores {rS} to effective address (rA + rB), clears the reserved bit, and sets CR0[EQ]. If the reserved bit is not set, the stwcx. instruction does not do a store; it leaves the reserved bit cleared and clears CR0[EQ]. Software must look at CR0[EQ] to see if the stwcx. was successful.

Name: stwu
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + {offset}. The contents of {rS} are stored into the word in memory addressed by EA. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: stwux
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The contents of {rS} are stored into the word in memory addressed by EA. EA is placed into {rA}.

Name: stwx
Description: EA is the sum ({rA}) + ({rB}). The contents of {rS} are stored into the word in memory addressed by EA.

Name: subf
Description: The sum ~({rA}) + ({rB}) + 1 is placed into {rD}. (equivalent to ({rB})-({rA}))

Name: subfc
Description: The sum ~({rA}) + ({rB}) + 1 is placed into {rD}. (equivalent to ({rB})-({rA}))

Name: subfe
Description: The sum ~({rA}) + ({rB}) + XER[CA] is placed into {rD}.

Name: subfic
Description: The sum ~({rA}) + {simm} + 1 is placed into {rD} (Equivalent to {simm}-({rA})).

Name: subfme
Description: The sum ~({rA}) + XER[CA] + (32)1 is placed into {rD}.

Name: subfze
Description: The sum ~({rA}) + XER[CA] is placed into {rD}.

Name: subi
Description: The difference ({rA}) - sign extended {simm}) is placed into {rD}

Name: subic
Description: The difference ({rA}) - sign extended {simm})) is placed into {rD}

Name: subic.
Description: The difference ({rA}) - sign extended {simm}) is placed into {rD}

Name: subis
Description: The difference ({rA}) - sign extended ({simm} << 16) is placed into {rD}

Name: sync
Description: The sync instruction provides an ordering function for the effects of all instructions executed by a given processor. Executing a sync instruction ensures that all instructions preceding the sync instruction appear to have completed before the sync instruction completes, and that no subsequent instructions are initiated by the processor until after the sync instruction completes.

Name: tlbie
Description: EA is the contents of {rB}. If the translation lookaside buffer (TLB) contains an entry corresponding to EA, that entry is made invalid (that is, removed from the TLB).

Name: tlbsync
Description: Executing a tlbsync instruction ensures that all tlbie instructions previously executed by the processor executing the tlbsync instruction have completed on all other processors.

Name: trap
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared arithmetically with the contents {rB} for TO[0, 1, 2]. The contents of {rA} are compared logically with the contents {rB} for TO[3, 4]. If any bit in the TO field is set and its corresponding condition is met by the result of the comparison, then the system trap handler is invoked.

Name: tw
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared arithmetically with the contents {rB} for TO[0, 1, 2]. The contents of {rA} are compared logically with the contents {rB} for TO[3, 4]. If any bit in {TO} is set and its corresponding condition is met by the result of the comparison, then the system trap handler is invoked.

Name: tweq
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared arithmetically with the contents {rB} for TO[0, 1, 2]. The contents of {rA} are compared logically with the contents {rB} for TO[3, 4]. If any bit in the TO field is set and its corresponding condition is met by the result of the comparison, then the system trap handler is invoked.

Name: twgti
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared arithmetically with the sign-extended value of the SIMM field for TO[0, 1, 2]. The contents of {rA} are compared logically with the sign-extended {simm} for TO[3, 4]. If any bit in the TO field is set and its corresponding condition is met by the result of the comparison, then the system trap handler is invoked.

Name: twi
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared arithmetically with the sign-extended value of the SIMM field for TO[0, 1, 2]. The contents of {rA} are compared logically with the sign-extended {simm} for TO[3, 4]. If any bit in {TO} is set and its corresponding condition is met by the result of the comparison, then the system trap handler is invoked.

Name: twlge
DescriptiveName: Trap if Logically Greater than or Equal
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared arithmetically with the contents {rB} for TO[0, 1, 2]. The contents of {rA} are compared logically with the contents {rB} for TO[3, 4]. If any bit in the TO field is set and its corresponding condition is met by the result of the comparison, then the system trap handler is invoked.

Name: twllei
DescriptiveName: Trap if Logically Less than or Equal Immediate
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared arithmetically with the sign-extended value of the SIMM field for TO[0, 1, 2]. The contents of {rA} are compared logically with the sign-extended {simm} for TO[3, 4]. If any bit in the TO field is set and its corresponding condition is met by the result of the comparison, then the system trap handler is invoked.

Name: twui
Description: The contents of {rA} are compared arithmetically with the sign-extended value of the SIMM field for TO[0, 1, 2]. The contents of {rA} are compared logically with the sign-extended {simm} for TO[3, 4]. If any bit in the TO field is set and its corresponding condition is met by the result of the comparison, then the system trap handler is invoked.

Name: xor
Description: The contents of {rS} are XORed with the contents of {rB} and the result is placed into {rA}.

Name: xori
Description: The contents of {rS} are XORed with ({uimm} & 0xFFFF) and the result is placed into {rA}.

Name: xoris
Description: The contents of {rS} are XORed with ({uimm} << 16) and the result is placed into {rA}.
